channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Luton North MP Sarah Owen halts in-person surgeries after security incidents
item.description: Labour MP Sarah Owen, who represents Luton North, has temporarily suspended her face-to-face constituency meetings following three separate security incidents within the past three weeks. In a stateme...

item.title: Tensions rise as Houthis target Israel amid widening Iran war and US troop buildup
item.description: The ongoing conflict in the Middle East has intensified after Yemen’s Houthi movement, which aligns with Iran, launched its first strikes on Israel. This escalation adds another front to the war that ...

item.title: Kenyan diplomats call for proper funding to handle emergencies involving citizens overseas
item.description: Kenyan envoys serving in various missions abroad have expressed deep frustration over limited or delayed budget allocations from the government, which they say has crippled their ability to assist cit...

item.title: Nine art collectives in Tanzania benefit from Feel Free Grant 2026 to boost creativity and cultural development
item.description: Nine Tanzanian art collectives have been granted a total of Sh188.4 million under the Feel Free Grant 2026, a programme managed by Nafasi Art Space to support the country’s growing creative sector. Th...

item.title: TPDC Takes Lead in Fuel Imports to Ensure Stable Prices in Tanzania
item.description: The Tanzanian government *ameamua* (decided) to task the Tanzania Petroleum Development Corporation (TPDC) with overseeing fuel imports, a move aimed at stabilizing prices and ensuring a consistent su...

item.title: Practical and emotional strategies for managing family heirlooms
item.description: The Globe and Mail article written by Matthew Hague explores the emotional, practical, and financial challenges of sorting through family heirlooms. It begins with the story of Alex Currie, who purcha...

item.title: Caster Semenya criticises IOC’s reintroduction of gender testing as harmful and unfair to women
item.description: South African Olympian and world champion Caster Semenya has strongly criticised the International Olympic Committee (IOC) for its new policy banning transgender women and athletes with differences in...

item.title: PIA Faces Severe Operational Challenges Amid Sharp Jet Fuel Price Surge
item.description: Chairman of the Pakistan International Airlines (PIA) Consortium, Arif Habib, has cautioned that the national flag carrier may soon be unable to sustain its operations following a massive 150 percent ...

item.title: Understanding Spousal Maintenance Under Nigerian Law
item.description: For Nigeria, many people dey think say alimony no exist or na oyinbo idea, but e no be so. This article explain how Nigerian law sabi handle matter wey concern spousal maintenance after divorce. Under...

item.title: WTO outlines how Middle East conflict may impact Africa’s trade and economy
item.description: The World Trade Organization (WTO) has raised concerns that the continuing conflict in the Middle East could slow down Africa’s recent trade recovery. During the WTO’s 14th Ministerial Conference in C...

item.title: When Love Abroad Turns into Regret: Stories of Relocation and Cultural Clash
item.description: This Guardian article explores the emotional and practical challenges faced by people who emigrated for love only to find that the new country, culture, or lifestyle did not suit them. It brings toget...

item.title: Government launches Sh2.6 billion project to strengthen climate adaptation in Tanzania and Zanzibar
item.description: The Government of Tanzania, through the Vice President’s Office, has set aside Sh2.6 billion to fund a climate change adaptation project spanning from 2025 to 2029. The initiative aims to strengthen c...

item.title: The role of sustainability in Tanzania’s private security industry
item.description: The article argues that Tanzania’s private security industry must embrace sustainability as an essential part of its operations. Traditionally, security has been seen only as the protection of people ...

item.title: ActionSA protests alleged coronation of Nigerian community leader in KuGompo City
item.description: ActionSA has announced plans to march in KuGompo City on Monday to oppose the reported coronation of Solomon Ogbonna Eziko, a Nigerian national who was allegedly installed under the traditional title ...

item.title: Pakistan hosts quadrilateral talks with Saudi Arabia, Turkiye and Egypt on Middle East conflict
item.description: Foreign ministers from Pakistan, Saudi Arabia, Turkiye and Egypt are meeting in Islamabad to discuss the worsening Middle East conflict and review the changing regional situation. The talks were conve...

item.title: Pakistan deepens diplomatic and strategic engagement with Türkiye and Egypt amid regional tensions
item.description: Pakistan has launched an intensive diplomatic push by hosting the foreign ministers of Türkiye and Egypt in Islamabad, with a focus on boosting cooperation and coordinating positions on fast‑evolving ...

item.title: Childcare Centres Transform Working Conditions for Market Mothers in Njombe
item.description: The introduction of childcare centres within marketplaces across Tanzania is bringing major relief to mothers working in the informal sector. Sparked by a 2022 directive from President Samia Suluhu Ha...

item.title: Finding the Best Sachertorte in Vienna’s Cafés
item.description: The article recounts the author’s delicious quest across Vienna to find the finest Sachertorte, Austria’s most iconic chocolate and apricot torte. While travelling through the city’s grand Ringstrasse...

item.title: Civilian Deaths Mount in Iran Amid Intensified Conflict, Activist Reports Show
item.description: According to reports from human rights activists and local observers, Iran has experienced one of the deadliest days for civilians since the outbreak of the current conflict in the region. A series of...

item.title: Lucas Torreira Hail Osimhen as One of World’s Top Three Players
item.description: Lucas Torreira, wey dey play midfield for Galatasaray and don follow play for Arsenal and Atlético Madrid before, don hail Nigerian striker Victor Osimhen as one of the best three footballers for the ...
